text.title: backlog-grooming-roadmapping Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backlog-grooming-roadmapping as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agents marking everything as Must priority.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Violates MoSCoW allocation and creates scope overrun risk. Enforce: Must cannot exceed 60% in the prompt explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Roadmap treated as a commitment: We promised Q2 for this feature.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Roadmap is a plan, not a promise. It must be updated as learning occurs. Treating it as a commitment prevents honest updates when circumstances change.

failure-mode.description: Too many P0/Must items (more than 3 simultaneous most important features).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If everything is most important, nothing is. Forces hard prioritization decisions before the roadmap is published.

failure-mode.description: Grooming session run as a status update meeting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Grooming focuses on prioritization, not updates. Status belongs in stand-up or async reports. Combining them dilutes both.
